title: Media
text:
Selected coverage of my research and activities in university news outlets, podcasts, and publications.


“Matthew Mair: Fighting climate change, economically” Virginia Tech Center for Communicating Science · January 2026


“Ph.D. Student Spotlight: Matthew Mair — Focused on the Future of the Environment” Department of Agricultural and Applied Economics, Virginia Tech · September 2025


“Management, MBA, and Economics students to present their research at ‘Student Research and Creative Endeavors’ event” Walker College of Business News, Appalachian State University · April 2024


Three photographs published in Sanctuary Literary Magazine Southern Regional Honors Conference · 2024


“The Honors College Hosts First Ever Honors Club Expo” Honors Wednesday Memo, Appalachian State University · December 2023


“Economics student studies the impact of sea level rise through undergraduate research experience” Walker College of Business News, Appalachian State University · May 2023

“016: App State at the United Nations Climate Change Conference” Podcast Find Your Sustain Ability, Appalachian State University · April 2023


“Honors College student and Chancellor’s Scholar accepted into the C2C REU Program” Honors Wednesday Memo, Appalachian State University · April 2023


“Honors College students present at the SHRC Annual Conference 2023” Honors Wednesday Memo, Appalachian State University · April 2023


“6 App State students observe world climate policymaking at UN climate conference” Walker College of Business News, Appalachian State University · January 2023


“App State students attend COP27, the 27th annual United Nations Climate Change Conference” Walker College of Business News, Appalachian State University · December 2022


“Honors College students travel to Egypt to attend the 27th annual Conference of Parties on climate change” Honors Wednesday Memo, Appalachian State University · November 2022


“Student leaders push for mental health wellness days for peers” The Appalachian, Appalachian State University · March 2021


“App State announces 2020–21 class of Chancellor’s Scholars” Appalachian Today, Appalachian State University · October 2020

title: Research
text:
with Greg Howard

Uses discrete choice analysis to identify the economic and behavioral factors that drive farmer participation in voluntary conservation programs in Eastern North Carolina.

In Preparation





with Klaus Moeltner and Martin Schnitzer

Applies machine learning methods to study conflict between ski tourers on shared Alpine slopes, with implications for recreation management and policy.

In Preparation




Examines how environmental stress interacts with labor-market conditions at origin to shape international migration flows, using bilateral panel data across 138 countries.

In Preparation





with Mario Ortez, Michael Smith, and Nicole Widmar

Uses natural language processing of online media to construct early-warning indicators of adverse crop conditions.

Under Review





with Zhenshan Chen

Develops a replicable framework for projecting property value impacts of proposed utility-scale solar facilities before construction, combining matched comparable sites, difference-in-differences estimation, and welfare translation.

In Preparation

title: Research
text:
Publications

Integrating human decision-making into a hydrological model to accurately estimate the impacts of agricultural policies

with M.R. Tapas, Greg Howard, R. Etheridge, and A. Peralta. Communications Earth & Environment 6, 412 (2025).


Published Version



Framework for Stakeholder-Driven Socio-Hydrological Modeling: Conceptual Foundations for Policy Development and Evaluation to Improve Ecosystem Health

with M.R. Tapas, R. Etheridge, and Greg Howard. In: Pandey, M., Umamahesh, N., Das, J., Pu, J.H. (eds) Hydrology and Hydrologic Modelling. HYDRO 2023. Lecture Notes in Civil Engineering, vol 410. Springer (2025).


Published Version

title: Matthew Mair
text:
I am a Ph.D. student in the Department of Agricultural and Applied Economics at Virginia Tech and an Interfaces of Global Change fellow at the Global Change Center. My research sits at the intersection of environmental economics, machine learning, and policy analysis, with a focus on understanding how human decision-making shapes environmental outcomes.
Fields: Environmental and Energy Economics, Applied Econometrics, Machine Learning
Email: mairmc@vt.edu
Google Scholar | GitHub | LinkedIn | ORCID



Ph.D., Agricultural and Applied Economics — Virginia Tech, 2024–Present

Interfaces of Global Change Fellow, Global Change Center

B.A., Economics (Environmental Economics and Policy); B.S., Political Science (International and Comparative Politics) — Appalachian State University, 2020–2024

University Honors College
Chancellor’s Scholarship (full-ride merit scholarship)
